Title: 1963 Studebaker Avanti
FunFact: It broke 29 speed records at the Bonneville Salt Flats.

Title: 1982 Jaguar XJS
FunFact: Famous for its aerodynamic flying buttresses behind the rear window.

Title: 1970 Chevrolet Nova
FunFact: One of the most popular platforms for custom street machines and drag racers.

Title: 1949 Chevrolet 3100
FunFact: Featuring 'Advance-Design' styling, it was America's number one selling truck.

Title: 1967 Ford Mustang Fastback
FunFact: Famously driven by Steve McQueen in the legendary movie chase scene in 'Bullitt'.

Title: 1973 Porsche 911 Carrera RS
FunFact: Widely considered the greatest classic 911 ever built, featuring the iconic ducktail spoiler.

Title: 1959 Cadillac Eldorado Biarritz
FunFact: Wore the tallest, most dramatic tailfins of any production car in history.

Title: 1971 Datsun 240Z
FunFact: Proved that Japan could manufacture world-class sports cars for an affordable price.

Title: 1961 Jaguar E-Type Series 1
FunFact: Enzo Ferrari called it 'the most beautiful car ever made' when it debuted.

Title: 1988 Ferrari Testarossa
FunFact: An absolute cultural symbol of the 1980s, recognizable by its huge side intake strakes.

Title: 1963 Aston Martin DB5
FunFact: The quintessential James Bond vehicle, debuting in the 1964 film 'Goldfinger'.

Title: 1974 BMW 2002 Turbo
FunFact: BMW's first turbocharged production car, designed to dominate European touring tracks.

Title: 1969 Dodge Charger R/T
FunFact: Features the iconic hidden headlight vacuum grille and full-width rear taillight loop.

Title: 1970 Plymouth Superbird
FunFact: Built strictly to lure racing legend Richard Petty back to Plymouth in NASCAR.

Title: 1963 Chevrolet Corvette Sting Ray
FunFact: Highly coveted because it was the only year the Corvette featured a Split Rear Window.

Title: 1972 Ford Bronco
FunFact: Designed originally as a rugged off-road competitor to the Jeep CJ-5 and International Scout.

Title: 1967 Volkswagen Type 2 Deluxe
FunFact: Known as the '21-Window Bus,' it features iconic skylight windows and a split windshield.

Title: 1975 Toyota Land Cruiser FJ40
FunFact: Famous globally for its legendary mechanical reliability and unstoppable off-road chassis.

Title: 1954 Mercedes-Benz 300 SL
FunFact: The fastest production car of its time, universally famous for its upward-opening Gullwing doors.

Title: 1966 AC Cobra 427
FunFact: An American monster V8 dropped into a tiny, lightweight British roadster chassis by Carroll Shelby.

Title: 1969 Chevrolet Camaro Z28
FunFact: Built specifically to qualify Chevrolet for the competitive SCCA Trans-Am road racing series.

Title: 1981 DeLorean DMC-12
FunFact: Features unpainted brushed stainless steel body panels and gained pop-culture immortality in 'Back to the Future'.

Title: 1970 Dodge Challenger R/T
FunFact: Released at the peak of the American muscle car era with vivid options like 'Plum Crazy'.

Title: 1994 Toyota Supra MK4
FunFact: The legendary 2JZ engine block is cast iron, meaning it can hold monstrous aftermarket power configurations.

Title: 1990 Nissan Skyline GT-R (R32)
FunFact: Nicknamed 'Godzilla' by journalists after utterly destroying standard track regulations.

Title: 1967 Alfa Romeo Spider Duetto
FunFact: Achieved massive fame after being driven by Dustin Hoffman in the 1967 film 'The Graduate'.

Title: 1970 Plymouth Hemi 'Cuda
FunFact: Extremely rare in convertible setups; some originals command millions at auctions.

Title: 1956 Porsche 356 Speedster
FunFact: A stripped-down racing convertible designed specifically for the sunny roads of Southern California.

Title: 1965 Shelby Mustang GT350
FunFact: All initial 1965 models were painted Wimbledon White with Guardsman Blue stripes.

Title: 1987 Buick Grand National GNX
FunFact: Affectionately nicknamed 'Darth Vader's Car' due to its sinister, completely blacked-out paint job.

Title: 1970 Ford Escort RS1600
FunFact: A legendary European rally championship slayer built with a high-revving race head.

Title: 1992 McLaren F1
FunFact: Features a central driver's seat and an engine bay lined with real gold foil to deflect intense exhaust heat.

Title: 1967 Chevrolet Corvette L88
FunFact: Only 20 models were made; the factory deliberately under-reported horsepower to discourage civilian sales.

Title: 1972 De Tomaso Pantera
FunFact: Combined stunning Italian wedge exotic styling with standard, easy-to-fix American Ford V8 muscle power.

Title: 1964 Lincoln Continental
FunFact: Famous for its clean, slab-sided body style and rear-hinged rear passenger 'Suicide Doors'.

Title: 1970 Pontiac GTO Judge
FunFact: Named after a running comedic catchphrase on the hit television variety show 'Laugh-In'.

Title: 1990 Honda NSX
FunFact: F1 racing legend Ayrton Senna directly helped tune its chassis handling during development tracks.

Title: 1969 Lamborghini Miura P400S
FunFact: Widely recognized by historians as the world's very first mid-engine exotic supercar.

Title: 1973 De Tomaso Longchamp
FunFact: A highly exclusive luxury grand tourer, only around 400 coupe examples were ever crafted.

Title: 1984 Ferrari 288 GTO
FunFact: Built to satisfy brutal Group B racing homologation requirements, but the series was banned before it raced.

Title: 1967 Shelby GT500 Eleanor
FunFact: Achieved worldwide cinematic pop-culture status as the ultimate star car heist target in 'Gone in 60 Seconds'.

Title: 1970 Plymouth GTX
FunFact: Marketed heavily to upscale enthusiasts as the dedicated 'Gentleman's Muscle Car'.

Title: 1997 Nissan R390 GT1
FunFact: Built specifically for Le Mans endurance racing; only a single road-legal factory version exists worldwide.

Title: 1955 Mercedes-Benz 190 SL
FunFact: Offered as a highly popular, more affordable roadster alternative to the legendary 300 SL Gullwing.

Title: 1968 Dodge Dart LO23
FunFact: Shipped from the factory without window regulators or sound deadening, built purely to dominate drag strips.

Title: 1971 Plymouth Road Runner
FunFact: Paid official royalties to Warner Bros. to use the cartoon character's exact trademark name and 'Beep-Beep' horn sound.

Title: 1985 Audi Sport Quattro
FunFact: Revolutionized rally sport forever by introducing all-wheel-drive grip technology into high-speed competition.

Title: 1969 Mercury Cougar Eliminator
FunFact: Mercury's direct answer to the Mustang Mach 1, offering premium tracking metrics with high muscle output.

Title: 1974 Lancia Stratos HF
FunFact: An extreme wedge-profile machine built from the ground up to sweep World Rally Championship titles.

Title: 1995 Porsche 911 GT2 (993)
FunFact: Nicknamed the 'Widowmaker' by race drivers due to its extreme power-to-weight ratio and unforgiving RWD layout.

Title: 1962 Alpine A110
FunFact: A lightweight fiberglass-bodied blue French icon that dominated tight, twisting mountain road rallies.

Title: 1969 Dodge Daytona
FunFact: The very first vehicle in NASCAR history to successfully cross the 200 mph threshold on track speed trials.

Title: 1970 Oldsmobile 442 W-30
FunFact: 4-4-2 stands for 4-barrel carburetor, 4-speed manual transmission, and dual (2) exhaust lines.

Title: 1987 Ferrari F40
FunFact: The final production supercar personally overseen and officially approved by company founder Enzo Ferrari.

Title: 1965 Shelby Cobra Daytona
FunFact: Only 6 coupe units were ever built by Carroll Shelby to dethrone Ferrari dominance at Le Mans racing circuits.

Title: 1971 AMC Javelin AMX
FunFact: Featured bold, exaggerated bulging front fender arches styled to match competitive Trans-Am track regulations.

Title: 1998 Subaru Impreza 22B STi
FunFact: Built to celebrate Subaru's 40th anniversary; all 400 limited units sold out in under 48 hours.

Title: 1957 Chevrolet Bel Air
FunFact: One of the earliest mass-market engines to hit the magical benchmark of 1 horsepower per cubic inch.

Title: 1968 Ford Mustang California Special
FunFact: A highly stylized regional edition that borrowed styling directly from Shelby prototype car configurations.

Title: 1971 Chevrolet Monte Carlo SS
FunFact: Combined a smooth luxury ride setup with massive big-block engine acceleration performance.

Title: 1983 Lancia 037
FunFact: The absolute last rear-wheel-drive vehicle to win the World Rally Championship before AWD systems took over.

Title: 1969 Chevrolet Chevelle SS 396
FunFact: One of the defining quintessential street machines of the classic American cruising culture era.

Title: 1973 Ford Falcon XB GT
FunFact: Achieved cinema history after being heavily customized into Mad Max's stealth black 'V8 Interceptor'.

Title: 1993 Vector W8 Twin Turbo
FunFact: Constructed with aerospace engineering composites, featuring an advanced cockpit matching fighter jets.

Title: 1953 Jaguar XK120 Drophead
FunFact: The 120 designation officially stood for its top tracking capability speed of 120 miles per hour.

Title: 1968 Pontiac Firebird 400
FunFact: Shipped out with twin functional hood scoops linked directly into an aggressive cold ram-air intake routing.

Title: 1972 Buick Riviera GS
FunFact: Universally famous for its polarizing, classical avant-garde rear 'Boat-Tail' styling design language.

Title: 1986 Porsche 959
FunFact: The most technologically high-tech street vehicle of its decade, featuring sequential turbos and smart AWD.

Title: 1966 Oldsmobile Toronado
FunFact: A mechanical milestone, breaking convention by pushing massive muscle big-block power through Front-Wheel-Drive.

Title: 1972 Chevrolet El Camino SS
FunFact: The classic automotive embodiment of the slogan 'Business in the front, party in the utility truck cargo bed'.

Title: 1999 Lamborghini Diablo GT
FunFact: A completely stripped-down, track-focused absolute beast of a supercar limited to only 80 production units.

Title: 1961 Lincoln Continental Sedan
FunFact: Introduced the iconic low-slung luxury profile with rear-opening doors that defined 1960s elite luxury.

Title: 1968 Plymouth Barracuda
FunFact: Famous for its massive, curved 'fastback bubble' rear glass window, the largest piece ever mounted on a car then.

Title: 1972 Dodge Charger SE
FunFact: Transitioned the aggressive muscle fastback platform into an elegant 'Special Edition' personal luxury cruiser.

Title: 1985 Mazda RX-7 GSL-SE
FunFact: Instead of traditional pistons, it utilized a high-revving triangular rotor spinning inside an oval chamber.

Title: 1969 Ford Mustang Boss 429
FunFact: The engine bay was so tight Ford had to contract out assembly modifications to physically slide the massive motor in.

Title: 1974 Plymouth Trail Duster
FunFact: Plymouth's rare, rugged entry into the full-size open-top utility tracking market to run against Blazer.

Title: 1992 Bugatti EB110 GT
FunFact: Built to honor Ettore Bugatti's 110th anniversary, packing four active turbos and an advanced carbon chassis.

Title: 1964 Ferrari 250 GTO
FunFact: One of the most highly valued collector artifacts on Earth, with single examples trading for over 70 million dollars.

Title: 1969 Pontiac Firebird Trans Am
FunFact: The very first year of the legendary Trans Am model name badge, dressed up in classic blue and white track colors.

Title: 1973 Chevrolet Corvette Stingray
FunFact: A historical styling transition model, pairing a soft body-colored urethane front bumper with a chrome rear bumper.

Title: 1989 Porsche 911 Turbo (930)
FunFact: The final year of the classic 930 generation, highly sought after because it introduced a modern 5-speed gearbox.

Title: 1969 Ford Torone Talladega
FunFact: Built with a specially lengthened, flush aerodynamic nose cone modification purely to win high-speed NASCAR battles.

Title: 1976 Cadillac Eldorado Convertible
FunFact: Promoted heavily by luxury dealers at the time as the literal 'Last of the Great American Convertible Fleets'.

Title: 1993 Toyota MR2 Turbo
FunFact: Dubbed the 'Baby Ferrari' due to its sleek mid-engine layout, rear pop-up engine layout, and snappy chassis handling.

Title: 1965 Pontiac GTO Tri-Power
FunFact: Packed three separate dual-barrel carburetors that opened up fully when you slammed down the gas pedal.

Title: 1969 AMC Hurst SC/Rambler
FunFact: Dressed up from the factory in an outrageous red, white, and blue paint scheme with an enormous hood scoop arrow.

Title: 1979 Pontiac Trans Am Firebird
FunFact: Wore the iconic, massive 'Screaming Chicken' graphic decal spread entirely across the layout of its front hood line.

Title: 1988 BMW M3 Evolution (E30)
FunFact: Built with wider boxed fender flares and a taller rear wing base to meet high-end touring track homologation rules.

Title: 1969 Ford Mustang Mach 1
FunFact: Introduced the classic performance option checklist, including matte black hood stripes, hood pins, and rear louvers.

Title: 1994 Nissan Pulsar GTI-R
FunFact: A miniature rally-bred hot hatch often referred to as the 'Baby Godzilla'.

Title: 1967 Pontiac GTO
FunFact: Features the highly recognizable split-grille design and stacked twin headlights.

Title: 1992 Lancia Delta HF Integrale Evo
FunFact: Dominated the World Rally Championship, securing an unmatched six consecutive manufacturer titles.

Title: 1970 Plymouth Duster 340
FunFact: A lightweight, fastback coupe designed to give maximum muscle performance for an affordable budget.

Title: 1985 Ford RS200
FunFact: A mid-engine, four-wheel-drive monster built by Ford of Europe specifically for the insane Group B rally era.

Title: 1968 Shelby Mustang GT500KR
FunFact: The 'KR' officially stood for 'King of the Road,' packing a severely under-rated high-torque powerplant.

Title: 1990 Mercedes-Benz 190E 2.5-16 Evo II
FunFact: Famous for its radical, aggressive factory body kit designed to slice through air on German DTM touring tracks.

Title: 1971 Plymouth GTX 440
FunFact: 1971 marked the final year the GTX existed as a standalone high-trim model before becoming an option package.

Title: 1978 Porsche 911 Turbo (930)
FunFact: Upgraded with a larger engine and the famous 'tea tray' rear spoiler to make room for its new intercooler layout.

Title: 1969 Dodge Super Bee
FunFact: Dodge's direct companion to the Plymouth Road Runner, sporting a distinct twin-looped front grille emblem setup.
